items.title: File streams and binary/text modes: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this C misconception? A null result from a read always means normal end-of-file and does not require checking stream error state.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. After a read stops, distinguish EOF from error with the stream state and validate the number of elements actually transferred.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. After a read stops, distinguish EOF from error with the stream state and validate the number of elements actually transferred.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned C correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: After a read stops, distinguish EOF from error with the stream state and validate the number of elements actually transferred.
A null result from a read always means normal end-of-file and does not require checking stream error state.

answer.explanation: After a read stops, distinguish EOF from error with the stream state and validate the number of elements actually transferred.

items.title: File streams and binary/text modes: executable application
items.prompt: Read up to 20 integers, persist them to a temporary text stream, rewind and read them back, then print their sum while checking and closing the stream.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards, security, and assessment review.

hints: Begin with the declared input and exact output contract, then implement the named language facet without relying on undefined behavior.

feedback.correct: The reference-aligned visible and hidden deterministic checks passed in the pinned isolated runtime.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check failed; inspect input validation, boundary behavior, and the named language rule without exposing hidden cases.

criteria.description: Compiles in the pinned runtime and passes every visible and hidden exact-output case.
Uses the target C or C++ language facet with declared bounds and without an out-of-scope workaround.

privateAuthorNotes: AI-assisted and human-unreviewed: formal exam eligibility is prohibited until independent technical and assessment approval.
Reference verification uses the digest-pinned local container with network=none, no package installation, a read-only source mount, and deterministic stdin/stdout.
Executable facet: owned temporary stream with checked write, rewind, read, and close

answer.explanation: The reference solution exercises owned temporary stream with checked write, rewind, read, and close under the bounded deterministic contract; it remains an AI-assisted draft pending human review.
